q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi></math> respectively be the modulus and amplitude of the complex number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>i</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>tan</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>8</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>r</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi></math> respectively be the modulus and amplitude of the complex number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>i</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>tan</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>8</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>r</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is equal to
solution:
Given: z=2-i2tan5π8

⇒z=21-itan5π8

⇒z=21-isin5π8cos5π8

⇒z=2cos5π8cos5π8-isin5π8

⇒z=2cosπ-3π8cosπ-3π8-isinπ-3π8

⇒z=2-cos3π8-cos3π8-isin3π8

⇒z=2cos3π8cos3π8+isin3π8

⇒z=2sec3π8ei·3π8

Now on comparing with z=zeiθ we get,

⇒θ=3π8, r=2sec3π8

subject: Mathematics
q: The area of the polygon, whose vertices are the non-real roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>z</mi><mo>¯</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mi>i</mi><msup><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is
question: The area of the polygon, whose vertices are the non-real roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>z</mi><mo>¯</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mi>i</mi><msup><mi>z</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is
solution:
z¯=iz2⇒x-iy=ix+iy2

⇒ x-iy=x2-y2i-2xy

i.e., x=-2xy and -y=x2-y2

⇒ x=0,y=-12 

When x=0; y=0,1 

When y=-12; x=±32

0,0 will be rejected as vertices would be non-real roots.

So, the vertices will be 0,1, 32,-12 and -32,--12

Hence, area of ∆=12×3×32=334

q: Let z1=2+3i and z2=3+4i. The set S=z∈C:z-z12-z-z22=z1-z22 represents a
question: Let z1=2+3i and z2=3+4i. The set S=z∈C:z-z12-z-z22=z1-z22 represents a
options: straight line with sum of its intercepts on the coordinate axes equals <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn></math>
hyperbola with the length of the transverse axis <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math>
straight line with the sum of its intercepts on the coordinate axes equals <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>18</mn></math>
solution:
Given,

z1=2+3i and z2=3+4i and the set

S=z∈C:z-z12-z-z22=z1-z22,

Now putting the value of z=x+iy, z1=2+3i and z2=3+4i in z-z12-z-z22=z1-z22 we get,

⇒(x-2)2+(y-3)2-(x-3)2-(y-4)2=1+1

⇒   x+y=7

Which is a straight line with intercept 7 on x & y axis, so sum of intercept will be 7+7=14.

q: Let w=zz¯+k1z+k2iz+λ(1+i),k1,k2∈ℝ. . Let Re(w)=0 be the circle C of radius 1 in the first quadrant touching the line y=1 and the y-axis. If the curve Im(w)=0 intersects C at A and B, then 30(AB)2 is equal to _______.
question: Let w=zz¯+k1z+k2iz+λ(1+i),k1,k2∈ℝ. . Let Re(w)=0 be the circle C of radius 1 in the first quadrant touching the line y=1 and the y-axis. If the curve Im(w)=0 intersects C at A and B, then 30(AB)2 is equal to _______.
solution:
Given,

w=zz¯+k1z+k2iz+λ(1+i),k1,k2∈ℝ. .

And Re(w)=0,

So, taking z=x+iy & z¯=x-iy

We get,

w=x2+y2+k1x+iy+k2ix+iy+λ(1+i)

⇒Rew=0⇒x2+y2+k1x-k2y+λ=0

And, Im(w)=0⇒k1y+k2x+λ=0

Now given Re(w)=0 circle is touching y=1 and y-axis, with radius 1, so we have equation of circle

x2+y2-2x-4y+4=0

So, on comparing with Re(w)=0 we get,

k1=-2,k2=4,λ=4

So, Im(w)=0⇒-2y+4x+4=0

And given -2 y+4 x+4=0 intersects the circle,

So, solving x2+y2-2x-4y+4=0 and -2 y+4 x+4=0 we get,

Intersecting point as A0,2 and B25,145

Hence, by distance formula we get,

30(AB)2=300-252+2-1452=24

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math><mi>z</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>C</mi></math> be such that <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>z</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If <math><mi>ω</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>z</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>z</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then:
question: Let <math><mi>z</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>C</mi></math> be such that <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>z</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If <math><mi>ω</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>z</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>z</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then:
solution:
Given z<1 and ω=5+3z51-z

⇒5ω1-z=5+3z

⇒5ω-5ωz=5+3z

⇒z=5ω-53+5ω

Using the given condition z=5ω-13+5ω<1


⇒5ω-1<3+5ω

⇒5ω-1<5ω+35

⇒ω-1<ω--35

We know that the locus of a complex number z1 satisfying z1-a=z2-b is the perpendicular bisector of the line segment joining the points a and b.

Hence, the locus of the point ω satisfying ω-1=ω--35 is the line x=1-352=15

Hence, Reω>15

⇒5Reω>1.

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of complex numbers $z$, satisfying $|z|=1$ and $\left|\frac{z}{\bar{z}}+\frac{\bar{z}}{z}\right|=1$, is :
question: The number of complex numbers $z$, satisfying $|z|=1$ and $\left|\frac{z}{\bar{z}}+\frac{\bar{z}}{z}\right|=1$, is :
solution: $\begin{aligned} & |z|=1 \\ & \left|\frac{z}{\bar{z}}+\frac{\bar{z}}{z}\right|=1 \\ & \Rightarrow\left|z^2+(\bar{z})^2\right|=1 \end{aligned}$ Let $z=x+i y$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow\left|(x+i y)^2+(x-i y)^2\right|=1 \\ & \Rightarrow\left|2 x^2-2 y^2\right|=1 \\ & \Rightarrow\left|x^2-y^2\right|=\frac{1}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow x^2-y^2=\frac{ \pm 1}{2} \\ & \text { and } x^2+y^2=1 \end{aligned}$ Case I: $x^2-y^2=\frac{1}{2}$ Case II: $x^2-y^2=-\frac{1}{2}$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> be the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>Im</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>></mo><mo>Im</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>)</mo></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>6</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> be the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>Im</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>></mo><mo>Im</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>)</mo></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>6</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
Given equation x2-x+2 has roots α & β,

So, α+β=1, αβ=2

⇒α4+β4=α2+β22-2α2β2

⇒α4+β4=α+β2-2αβ2-2α2β2

⇒α4+β4=1-42-2×4

⇒α4+β4=1

It is given that, α is a root of x2-x+2.

⇒α2-α+2=0

⇒α2=α-2

⇒α4=α2+4-4α

⇒α4=α-2+4-4α

⇒α4=2-3α

Now, α6-5α2=α2α4-5

⇒α6-5α2=α-22-3α-5

⇒α6-5α2=α-2-3α-3

⇒α6-5α2=-3α2-α-2

⇒α6-5α2=-3α-2-α-2

⇒α6-5α2=12

⇒α6+α4+β4-5α2=13

q:
Let z1 and z2 be any two non-zero complex numbers such that 3z1=4z2. If z=3z12z2+2z23z1 then maximum value of z is

Note: In actual paper value of z was asked. Hence, none of the options given were correct. So we have modified the question as well as options.
question:
Let z1 and z2 be any two non-zero complex numbers such that 3z1=4z2. If z=3z12z2+2z23z1 then maximum value of z is

Note: In actual paper value of z was asked. Hence, none of the options given were correct. So we have modified the question as well as options.
solution:
Let arg3z12z2=θ, and we know that, if the argz=α, then arg1z=-α.

∴ arg2z23z1=-θ

Also, we know that a complex number w can be expressed as w=wcosα+isinα, where α is the argument of the complex number.

∴ z=32z1z2cosθ+isinθ+23z2z1cosθ-isinθ

Given, 3z1=4z2,  ⇒z1z2=43,

⇒z=32×43cosθ+isinθ+23×34cosθ-isinθ

⇒z=2cosθ+isinθ+12cosθ-isinθ

⇒z=52cosθ+32sinθi

∴ z=254cos2θ+94sin2θ

Now, using sin2θ+cos2θ=1, we get

z=254cos2θ+941-cos2θ

⇒z=4cos2θ+94

And, we know that the maximum value of cosθ is 1.
∴ zmax=4+94=52.
